spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Reorder imports to group third-party imports before project-specific imports.
Replace tab indentation with spaces to conform to standard Java formatting conventions.
Reformat method signature and body to use spaces and align parameters.
Reformat method signature to align path variables on separate lines.
Reformat POST method signature and body to use spaces and align parameters.
Reformat method body logic to use spaces and chain builder calls on separate lines.
Reformat the String.format call in toString() to use a single line for the format string.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.after:
return String.format("Course [id=%s, name=%s, description=%s, steps=%s]",
				id, name, description, steps);

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Refactor the null-check logic in equals() to be more concise.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.after:
return other.id == null;
		} else return id.equals(other.id);

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace the deprecated @Component annotation with the more specific @Service annotation for a service layer class.
Add the 'final' modifier to a static list field to enforce immutability of the reference.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.before: private static List<Student> students = new ArrayList<>();
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.after: private static final List<Student> students = new ArrayList<>();

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Move the SecureRandom field initialization from a method-local or late position to a class-level final field for better encapsulation.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.after: private final SecureRandom random = new SecureRandom();

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace Arrays.asList() with List.of() to use the modern immutable list factory method introduced in Java 9.
Remove the unused import for java.util.Arrays after replacing Arrays.asList with List.of.
Simplify the null check logic in retrieveCourses using a ternary operator instead of an if-block.
Replace JUnit 4 import with JUnit 5 import for the @Test annotation.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: org.junit.Test is not available in JUnit 5; tests will fail to compile if JUnit 4 dependencies are removed or if the project enforces JUnit 5 platform.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 @RunWith annotation with JUnit 5 @ExtendWith annotation.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: @RunWith is a JUnit 4 specific annotation and is ignored or causes errors in JUnit 5 platform; Spring Boot 3 requires JUnit 5 extensions.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 SpringRunner import with JUnit 5 SpringExtension import.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: SpringRunner is part of the JUnit 4 integration; SpringExtension is the JUnit 5 equivalent required for Spring Boot 3.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Migrate JUnit 4 imports to JUnit 5 (Jupiter) imports.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: JUnit 4 classes (org.junit.Test, org.junit.Assert) are not part of the JUnit 5 API; tests will fail to compile or run if JUnit 5 is the active test engine without these imports.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 @RunWith(SpringRunner.class) with JUnit 5 @ExtendWith(SpringExtension.class).
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: Spring Boot 3 requires JUnit 5. The @RunWith annotation is JUnit 4 specific and ignored by the JUnit 5 engine, causing the Spring context not to load and tests to fail.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Remove deprecated 'secure' attribute from @WebMvcTest annotation.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: The 'secure' attribute was removed in Spring Security 6 (used by Spring Boot 3). Keeping it causes a compilation error.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Add @WithMockUser to handle Spring Security 6 default strict security.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: Spring Security 6 enables strict security by default. Without @WithMockUser (or disabling security), requests in tests will be rejected with 401/403, causing test assertions to fail.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Replace Arrays.asList with List.of for immutable list creation.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: While Arrays.asList still compiles, List.of is the preferred modern Java 17+ idiom for immutable lists. This is a stylistic modernization.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Update test data description string to match expected output.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: The test assertion expects '10Steps' (no space) in the JSON response, so the mock data and expected string must match this format.

spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.general_idea: Reformat code indentation and line breaks to match modern style.
spring-boot-rest-services-with-unit-and-.why_exists: Cosmetic change to improve readability and consistency with project style guides.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/main/java/.general_idea: Remove explicit @EnableAutoConfiguration annotation as its functionality is included in @SpringBootApplication.
Remove unused import for EnableAutoConfiguration after removing the annotation.
Enable the SecurityConfig class by uncommenting the @Configuration annotation.
Prefix in-memory passwords with {noop} to satisfy Spring Security 5.8+ requirement for password encoding identifiers.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/main/java/.why_exists: Spring Security 5.8+ throws an IllegalArgumentException at runtime if a password is provided without an encoding identifier (like {noop}, {bcrypt}, etc.) when a PasswordEncoder bean is present.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Update import for LocalServerPort to match Spring Boot 3 package structure.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: The class org.springframework.boot.context.embedded.LocalServerPort was removed in Spring Boot 3; using the old import causes a compile error.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Add explicit throws declaration for JSONException to satisfy compiler requirements.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: JSONAssert.assertEquals throws JSONException; without the throws clause, the code fails to compile.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Add import for JSONException to support the new throws declaration.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: The JSONException class is not imported by default; without this import, the throws clause causes a compile error.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Update test data string to match expected output format.
Update test input data to match expected output format.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.before: Course course = new Course("Course1", "Spring", "10 Steps", Arrays

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Remove deprecated 'secure' attribute from @WebMvcTest annotation to comply with Spring Boot 3.0 API changes.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: The 'secure' attribute was removed from @WebMvcTest in Spring Boot 3.0; retaining it causes a compilation error.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Add @WithMockUser annotation to handle security context requirements in Spring Boot 3.0 tests.
spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.0 enforces stricter security defaults; without a mock user, requests may fail with 401/403 or security context initialization errors.

spring-boot-rest-services/src/test/java/.general_idea: Update test data string from '10 Steps' to '10Steps' to match updated mock object construction.
Update expected JSON string in test assertion to match the new '10Steps' description format.

spring-boot-web-application/src/main/jav.general_idea: Add a NoOpPasswordEncoder to in-memory authentication configuration to satisfy Spring Security 5.8+ requirement for explicit password encoding.
spring-boot-web-application/src/main/jav.why_exists: Spring Security 5.8+ (used in Spring Boot 3) throws an IllegalArgumentException at runtime if no PasswordEncoder is configured for in-memory authentication, as it no longer allows plain text passwords by default.
